blocks.content: Form a defensive square!
Forty soldiers quickly formed a circle,
understanding that in a situation where the enemy couldn't be identified, preventing isolation and defeat was paramount.
Kuan finally spotted the enemy.
About 20 meters away, a dark curtain shimmered before white masks suddenly flipped open, revealing their wearers.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: What is this?
Figures wearing owl-like masks swirled around a point before forming a triangular formation.
Shing!
Thirty soldiers drew their swords in unison, but the sound was singular.
The sight of them standing in the same pose, aiming diagonally, brought back information about a particular group Kuan had learned about during commander training.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Could it be...
The Wind Guards, the most formidable swordsmanship group known.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Why are the Wind Guards here?
The Wind Guards were the imperial guards of Empress Teraje of the Kashan Empire. Their presence meant only one thing: the cult leader Kointira was under Teraje's protection.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: What are you plotting, Kashan?

blocks.speaker: Wind Guard
blocks.content: You'll find out in the afterlife...

blocks.speaker: Wind Guard
blocks.content: You will know then.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Argh!
A soldier from the second company screamed. Even Kuan felt the tremendous murderous intent. No ordinary soldier, even a veteran, could stand against such a force.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Damn!
Kuan dashed forward, and the Wind Guards dispersed like the wind, only to regroup rapidly.
Dozens of swords created thousands of afterimages as Kuan's movements seemed to defy gravity.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Damn. How many centers of gravity are there?
The skill of the Wind Guards was astonishing. They were humans who seemed to implement fluid dynamics with their bodies. Losing all sense of direction, it felt like fighting in zero gravity.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: But what truly surprised was the Wind Guards.

blocks.speaker: Wind Guard
blocks.content: It's taking too long. How many swings have there been?

blocks.speaker: Wind Guard
blocks.content: Around 23,000.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: It was rare for anyone to withstand over 1,000 strikes from the Wind Guards. Essentially, they could grind down any being in under a second.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: How playful can God be?
If the world were perfect, all creatures would be happy as lesser beings. However, nature always creates a 0.1% mutation. That 0.1% makes the rest of the 99.9% seem trivial.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: A being born from chance... specially chosen.

blocks.speaker: Wind Guard
blocks.content: This man is a genius.
As soon as the thought crossed their minds, the Wind Guards expanded like wings, then regrouped as a fierce wind swirling around Kuan.

blocks.speaker: Wind Guard
blocks.content: To kill a genius.
The Wind Guards' blades occupied all space. They were so fast it almost felt slow.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Charge! Charge!
At that moment, the company members rushed in. Upon entering the Wind Guards' radius, they were shredded like meat caught in machinery.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Kuan's escape from the sword net was almost instinctual.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: This is madness...!
The oldest member of the company, an old man named Kovira, passed by Kuan without a chance for a farewell, but his smiling eyes conveyed a thousand words.

blocks.speaker: Kovira
blocks.content: Survive, Captain.
Watching his company being slaughtered in an instant, Kuan understood why they had to die.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: I see. At least one must escape.
It was necessary to inform the higher-ups about Kashan's involvement. Otherwise, more search teams would fall into the Wind Guards' trap.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: This is the best course. If anyone should survive, I have the highest chance.
As Kuan propelled himself away with external gravity, he coldly watched the swirling black storm in the darkness.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: He hadn't even shared a day or a look with the soldiers, yet they had all departed from this world.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Why...

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: He felt something hot inside him. A massive flame had ignited from a place he thought had been extinguished to ashes.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Why the hell! Damn it all!
As Kuan exploded with external gravity, the Wind Guards, like feathers, regrouped in a triangular formation 20 meters away.

blocks.speaker: Wind Guard
blocks.content: None of you think about returning alive.
The Wind Guards did not respond. Their minds were racing with the unexpected situation.

blocks.speaker: Wind Guard
blocks.content: The mission has failed.
Or, it almost did.

blocks.speaker: Wind Guard
blocks.content: If Kuan had decided to flee, the Wind Guards would not have been able to catch him.
The trivial lives of forty had created an unprecedented precedent against the mighty Wind Guards.
The leader of the Wind Guards slowly raised a hand and removed her mask, revealing a woman's face with narrow, sharp eyes beneath thin, sparse eyebrows.

blocks.speaker: Wind Guard
blocks.content: This is the first time a living being has seen the face of the Wind Guards.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: So what? Expecting me to be grateful?

blocks.speaker: Wind Guard
blocks.content: Join the Wind Guards. We will lead you.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: I refuse.
The woman's head tilted slightly.

blocks.speaker: Wind Guard
blocks.content: It's unfortunate about your subordinates. But a new future awaits you. Don't bury your talent in the past.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Don't be mistaken. My men did their duty as soldiers. The foolish ones are those commanders who can't control their emotions.

blocks.speaker: Wind Guard
blocks.content: Then why refuse?

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Ever wondered how embarrassing it is? Claiming to be the strongest while you mob up to slash?

blocks.speaker: Wind Guard
blocks.content: Then it's a breakdown.
At the same time as the woman put on her owl mask, the thirty Wind Guards dispersed like wind and swooped towards Kuan.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Wait. I'll take at least one with me.
How could he face the underworld without even a companion to accompany him if he deserted the expectations of his subordinates?
Kuan spread external gravity all around as he dove into the storm created by the Wind Guards.
The movements created by the masters of external gravity were almost like an art form beyond combat.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: I see. The answer was surprisingly simple.
Kuan leaped over the wall.
He was able to do it because he hadn't intended to survive.
This time, even the Wind Guards had to tense up.
The forest shook with the eerie sound of dozens of swords.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Is a sword this beautiful?
Kuan's sword split the path.
A Wind Guard's head detached from its body, and simultaneously another's heart ceased as it was pierced.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Two. That should be enough to pay for the meal.
Whoosh!
As the Wind Guards realized the death of their comrades, the turbulence exploded like a storm.
The simultaneous death of two members was a very rare event in the history of the Wind Guards.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: However, they didn't kill him.
Instead, they sliced off his Achilles tendon like a sliver of fish flesh.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Argh!
Kuan screamed, not from pain but because everything that made him who he was, including his recent enlightenment, had vanished with the 30 grams of flesh that had been stripped away.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: The nickname 'Sorcerer of the Battlefield' had come from his miraculous skill with the sword, which could take an opponent's neck in any situation, but it was also backed by phenomenal agility and mobility.
Without the Achilles tendon, there was no genius. He was no longer the Sorcerer of the Battlefield.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Ugh...
Kuan couldn't stand.
He couldn't even try.
Yet, he reached out and crawled up the slope.
The eerie sound of the Wind Guards swirling around him echoed.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: The voice of 28 men uttering a single syllable each would be unforgettable for a lifetime.

blocks.speaker: Wind Guard
blocks.content: You've become a mere bug.
Kuan kept crawling.
Death was not frightening because he had lost everything.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: But he couldn't give up on life after abandoning the 40 members of his unit.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Struggle like a bug.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Bark like a dog.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Kill! Just kill!

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Confess to your master. Tell them the Wind Guards have spared you. Wag your tail and snitch.
As the Wind Guards' wind grew fiercer, the sound of flames crackling could be heard.
To Kuan, it was as if the world was burning.
Burying his face in the ground, he clenched his fists.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: He could no longer endure.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: He couldn't prevent his life's beliefs from plummeting into the abyss.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Argh!
Kuan's scream echoed in the now silent Ghost Forest.
Kuan opened his eyes.
His face was still expressionless, but sweat dotted his forehead.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Damn.
He felt as if he was pinned down, unable to move a finger.
This always happened after dreaming of the Wind Guards.
He had to lie still like a corpse for 10 minutes, just as on the day 10 years ago when his Achilles tendon was severed.
After barely escaping the Ghost Forest, Kuan lost consciousness and was urgently transported back to Thormia.
He reported that Teraje was protecting the Dark Order, but as expected, it had no impact on the political situation.
The search battalion surrounded the mountain range for about a month in a stagnant local war before disbanding.
There seemed to be some sort of agreement with the Kashan Empire, but that was a matter for the politicians, not something Kuan was involved in.
Kuan could not become a hero.
The event of that day vanished into the annals of history without even a mention of the Wind Guards.
The military had offered him a position as a cavalry battalion commander—a two-rank special promotion—but it was clearly intended to silence him.
Kuan chose to discharge.
His mind was already too shattered to wield a sword.
For a while, he lived like a hermit.
He couldn't even dream of suicide as he thought of his subordinates, nor did he have any proper supporters.
Then one day, at the suggestion of his only friend, Kiyo Elis, he took a position at the Kaizen School of Swordsmanship.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: 'I can move now.'
Kuan climbed out of bed, limping to the window.
Pulling back the curtain, the morning sun shone on his old desk.
Numerous papers were scattered about.
Psychological Views on the Phenomenon of Divine Transcendence,
In the corner of the desk were notes Kuan had scribbled with his own thoughts, which he now swept away with his hand.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Despite gathering all material on divine transcendence, he still hadn't reached it.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Let's assume for a moment that it's possible to move the body with the power of will.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: But what exactly is will?

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: He was still limping.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: No matter how hard he tried to walk properly, it just didn't work.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: 'Ogent Rian.'

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Who could have predicted?

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: That a boy with no apparent talent would achieve what Kuan, recognized as a genius, could not.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: 'I hope it's not just becoming a fool.'
Kuan shook his head.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: He knew the truth.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: His sword was already broken.
The moment his Achilles tendon snapped, the god of swords had also turned his gaze away from him.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: 'Another year has ended.'
Today was the last day of the second half at the school of swordsmanship.
The subject Kuan was in charge of was wrestling, among the combat evaluations.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Even if Rian could perform divine transcendence, he couldn't withstand the enhanced strength of Schema users.
It was probably going to be another miserable day for him, but he was curious about the half-year's achievements nonetheless.
Tess entered the locker room with the female cadets in her group.
Wrestling required close physical contact, but since there was no distinction between men and women in the swordsmanship school, everyone had to remove their tops during evaluations.
Women were allowed to wear tank tops, however.
Tess struggled to put on the tank top.
Not only was her frame large, but her ample bust meant she almost had to cram her breasts in by force.
